title: Why Aren’t American Records Worth More Money?!
description:
Why can an athlete break an American record and still barely get paid?

In this Night Swim Podcast segment, we break down the financial reality of elite swimming and why some of the sport’s biggest accomplishments don’t translate into meaningful money.

We discuss:

Prize money in swimming
Sponsorship problems
Why other sports reward stars better
The business side of Olympic swimming

This conversation exposes a massive issue in the sport.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Most Swim Training Is COMPLETE Bullsh*t
description:
Is most swim training actually ineffective?

In this Night Swim Podcast segment, we break down why so much traditional swimming training may be outdated, inefficient, or completely disconnected from actual race performance.

We dive into:

Junk yardage and wasted volume
Why swimmers plateau for years
The disconnect between training and racing
What elite sprinters are doing differently

This conversation challenges everything swimmers are taught growing up.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Would Van Mathias Take the Enhanced Games Deal?
description:
What happens when you ask an elite swimmer the question everyone is thinking about?

In this Night Swim Podcast segment, American record holder Van Mathias is asked point blank whether he’d compete in the Enhanced Games—and the conversation gets real fast.

We dive into:

The growing buzz around the Enhanced Games
Why athletes are even considering it
The money, ethics, and pressure involved
How swimming could change forever if this becomes mainstream

Would YOU do it?
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Van Mathias Talks About Sodium Bicarb Farts and What Not!
Van Mathias Makes a BOLD Prediction! 57 in the 100 Breast?!
description:
Is a 57-second 100 breaststroke actually coming?

In this Night Swim Podcast segment, Van Mathias makes a bold claim—that 57 is the next barrier in the 100 breast. With sprint swimming evolving fast, the question isn’t if… it’s when.

We break down:

What it would take to go 57
How technique and power are evolving in breaststroke
Whether today’s swimmers are already close
What this means for the future of sprint breast

Is this prediction crazy… or right on the money?
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Van Mathias REVEALS Low Yardage Training That Broke the 50 Breast AR
description:
Van Mathias (IU Director of Operations & American Record Holder in the 50 Breaststroke) joins Night Swim for a raw, in-depth conversation about his incredible comeback!

From working full-time, training on low volume/high intensity (heavily inspired by Cam McEvoy), crushing weighted pull-ups & Olympic lifts, to setting the American Record in Norway — Van breaks down exactly how he did it.

**Topics Covered:**
• His exact weekly training split (speed endurance, dryland, lifting)
• How he went from “just staying in shape” to American Record holder in months
• Sodium bicarb experience, mindset on borrowed time, and racing philosophy
• Training with Coach John, the guinea pig experiments, and what’s next (Pan Pacs, 57 in the 100 breast?)
• Life as a swimmer + Director of Ops (feeding 90 athletes, jail cell office, etc.)

Van is one of the most interesting stories in American swimming right now. Don’t miss this one!

Timestamps:
0:00 Intro + Elvis Solo Hosting
1:05 Van Mathias Joins (Director of Ops Life)
4:20 Feeding 90 Athletes is Harder Than You Think
8:10 “Swimming Was My Side Hustle… Now It’s Not”
10:45 The Moment Everything Changed (World Trials)
13:20 Training Plan Inspired by Cam McEvoy
16:05 Low Yardage, High Speed Training Explained
18:40 From 235 lbs to Elite Sprint Shape
21:15 “This is My Extra Life” Mindset
24:30 What a Real Sprint Practice Looks Like
27:10 Speed Endurance Sets That Destroy You
30:20 Why This Training Makes You Want to Throw Up
33:05 Weighted Pull-Ups + Strength Training
36:10 Olympic Lifting vs Swim-Specific Training
39:00 Norway Meet Experience + Travel Strategy
43:15 Time Zone Impact on Performance
47:10 What’s Next: Pro Series + Pan Pacs
49:20 “I Think I Can Go 57 in the 100 Breast”
51:00 Olympics Talk (Why He’s Not Thinking About It Yet)
54:10 NCAA vs Pro Training Differences
58:30 Mid-Distance → Sprint Transition
1:02:00 Ferrari vs Lawnmower (Best Coaching Advice)
1:05:20 “Guinea Pig” Training Experiments
1:08:30 Why Traditional Training is Wrong
1:11:00 How He Actually Built 100 Speed
1:14:20 The Secret: Speed First, Then Endurance
1:17:00 Why Most “Fast Sets” Are Fake
1:20:10 Puking During Practice (Real Talk)
1:23:00 Sodium Bicarb: Does It Actually Work?
1:28:00 Ice Baths vs Hot Showers Debate
1:30:40 Pan Pacs Events + Expanding Beyond Breaststroke
1:34:20 Racing Backstroke??
1:37:00 Why Swimming Has to Stay Fun
1:40:10 Final Thoughts + What’s Coming Next

Subscribe for more deep dives with elite swimmers!

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Why Swimming Is Behind Every Other Sport?!
description:
Is swimming stuck in the past?

In this Night Swim Podcast segment, we break down why swimming feels “backwards” compared to other major sports—and what that means for athletes, fans, and the future of the sport.

We cover:

Outdated systems and structures
Lack of innovation in competition formats
Why other sports are more engaging
What swimming needs to change NOW

This is a raw look at where the sport stands today.🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Swimming Is Losing BILLIONS… Here’s Why
description:
Why is swimming leaving BILLIONS on the table?

In this Night Swim Podcast segment, we break down the massive gap between swimming and other major sports when it comes to money, media, and growth.

We dive into:

Why swimming struggles with monetization
The lack of entertainment and storytelling
Missed opportunities in media and sponsorships
What other sports do WAY better

If swimming wants to grow, this is the conversation that needs to happen.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd.

title: Why Cam McEvoy Defended Bob Bowman
description:
Did Cam McEvoy just call us out?

After our take on Bob Bowman, Cam pushed back—and it sparked a bigger conversation about coaching, criticism, and respect in the sport.

In this episode, we break down:

What Cam actually said
Why Bowman’s coaching style divides people
Where critique crosses the line
How elite swimmers view coaching differently

This is one of the most honest conversations we’ve had about coaching in swimming.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Do Sprinters Actually Know What They’re Doing?!
description:
Are sprinters actually training with intention… or just guessing?

In this Night Swim Podcast segment, we break down a brutal claim about sprint swimming—that many athletes don’t fully understand what they’re doing in the water. From technique to training philosophy, we explore why sprinting may be more misunderstood than any other event.

We dive into:

Why sprint training lacks clarity
The disconnect between feel vs science
How elite sprinters actually think about speed
What swimmers SHOULD be focusing on

This one will challenge how you think about sprinting.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Night Swim is always right! B Finals Debate: Elvis Saw This Coming
description:
Did Elvis just call this weeks ago?

In this Night Swim Podcast segment, we revisit the B finals debate—and the prediction that they’d come back. With momentum shifting and more coaches speaking out, it’s starting to look like this wasn’t just a hot take… it might’ve been inevitable.

We break down:

Why B finals were removed in the first place
Why the backlash has been so strong
How this impacts team scoring and athlete development
Whether the decision could actually be reversed

Was this a lucky guess… or a perfect read on where swimming is headed?
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Would a S&C Coach Really Dominate SEC Swimming?
description:
Could a S&C coach walk in and dominate swimming?

This bold claim from Cam McEvoy has the entire swimming world debating what actually matters in coaching.

In this Night Swim Podcast segment, we break down:

Why sprint swimming might be more like track than we think
Whether traditional swim coaching is overcomplicating things
The role of strength, power, and speed vs volume
What this means for the future of elite swim training

Is this take completely ridiculous… or uncomfortably true?

If you’re a swimmer or coach, this conversation will challenge how you think about training.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: This Cam McEvoy Comment About Enhanced Games Is WILD
description:
Did Cam McEvoy just hint at competing in the controversial Enhanced Games?

In this Night Swim Podcast segment, we break down one of the most shocking moments from the conversation — where McEvoy seemingly leaves the door open to the idea of enhanced competition.

We dive into:

What Cam actually said about the Enhanced Games
Why this idea is gaining traction in elite sports
The potential impact on swimming and Olympic competition

title: This Cam McEvoy Training Debate Is Breaking Swimming
description:
Why are so many swimmers and coaches getting heated about Cam McEvoy’s training approach?

In this episode of the Night Swim Podcast, we break down the controversy surrounding one of the most talked-about sprint training philosophies in modern swimming. Cam McEvoy has completely flipped traditional sprint training on its head — focusing on ultra-specific, high-quality work instead of the high-volume grind most swimmers are used to.

So why does it make people so uncomfortable?

We dive into:

Why McEvoy’s approach challenges everything coaches believe about sprint training
The emotional reaction from the swimming community
Whether less volume could actually produce faster swimmers
What this means for the future of sprint events

Is this the future of sprinting… or just a trend that’s triggering everyone?

If you’re a swimmer or coach trying to figure out what actually works, this conversation is a must-watch.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Cam McEvoy EXPOSES Why Traditional Swim Training Is Broken (World Record Secrets)
description:
Cam McEvoy joins the Night Swim Podcast fresh off breaking the world record in the 50 freestyle — and what he reveals about training, sprinting, and the future of swimming will change how you think about the sport.

In this episode, Cam dives deep into:

Why traditional swim training might be outdated
The truth about sprint vs endurance training
His unconventional approach to becoming the fastest man in the world
Whether he’ll return to the 100 freestyle
The controversy around new training methods and why people are so resistant to change
Enhanced Games, performance debates, and the future of elite competition

This isn’t just an interview — it’s a blueprint for where swimming is headed next.

If you're a swimmer, coach, or fan of elite performance, this is a must-watch.
